mission.title: kserve: Support  for PySpark 2.4.4 Mllib
mission.description: Support  for PySpark 2.4.4 Mllib . Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current kserve deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kserve version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
helm list -n kserve 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kserve installation.

steps.title: Review kserve configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kserve configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
kubectl get configmap -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kserve
```
Hi, our current pipeline and models are implemented using PySpark Mllib. After looking at the examples I realize we currently don't support PySpark Mllib.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support  for PySpark 2.4.4 Mllib
steps.description:
spark.mllib supports model export to Predictive Model Markup Language (PMML), this is to show an example how you can serve a pmml model on KFServing exported fro spark mllib

*(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*:
1. Please confirm that if this PR changes any image versions, then that's the sole change this PR makes.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade kserve to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kserve:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kserve kserve/kserve --namespace kserve
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve
helm list -n kserve
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
kubectl get events -n kserve --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support  for PySpark 2.4.4 Mllib " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
spark.mllib supports model export to Predictive Model Markup Language (PMML), this is to show an example how you can serve a pmml model on KFServing exported fro spark mllib

*(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*:
1.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kserve installed or the issue environment reproducible.
